question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the corresponding angles measures 65°. What is the measure of its corresponding angle?
hints: Corresponding angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal are equal.
solution: Corresponding angles are equal, so the other angle is also 65°.

question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the alternate interior angles measures 110°. What is the measure of the other alternate interior angle?
hints: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.

question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 72°. Its co-interior (same-side interior) angle is what measure?
hints: Co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary — they add to 180°.

question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is (3x + 15)° and its alternate exterior angle is (5x − 25)°. Find x.
hints: Alternate exterior angles are equal when lines are parallel.

question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Angle 1 = (3x + 15)° and angle 2 = (2x + 25)° are co-interior angles. Find x and the measure of both angles.
